activeGoal: ELM-005: 将当前插件界面的“对话过程展示”升级为类似 VS Code Copilot 的会话式过程体验，以更接近对话消息流的方式呈现用户输入、系统响应、生成中状态与过程推进，让用户能够直观理解正在处理什么、处理到了哪里、最终产出了什
designSummary: ELM-005 follows a brownfield-first refinement of ELM-004. Extend the existing Chrome extension sidepanel implementation instead of creating a new chat stack or changing backend protocols. The implementation boundary remains extension/src/sidepanel/App.tsx, reasoningTimeline.ts, reasoningTimelineView.tsx, styles.css, and useRunHistory.ts. Introduce a Conversation Shell responsibility at the App.tsx layer so the UI is reorganized around a conversation-first flow: user message, assistant processing state, progressively forming assistant reply, summarized/collapsible process layer, and clear completion state. Reuse the existing reasoning timeline, event presentation, stream UX, and history reuse seams to map raw NormalizedRunEvent data into stable user-facing stage summaries with low noise and low jitter.
designDecisions: Decision: Select extension/src/sidepanel/App.tsx as the primary ownership seam for ELM-005 because the requirement expands from ELM-004's reasoning timeline refinement into whole-sidepanel conversation orchestration. The top-level sidepanel shell already owns prompt submission, live run state, question handling, and completion banners, so the Copilot-like conversation flow must be composed there rather than introduced as a parallel UI path.

Decision: Reuse extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts as the event-to-conversation mapping seam for ELM-005. It already converts NormalizedRunEvent items into aggregated readable units, so it is the correct brownfield boundary for evolving raw event aggregation into stable stage summaries and assistant-turn conversation models.

Decision: Reuse extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx and extension/src/sidepanel/styles.css as the rendering seam for ELM-005 because they already own buffered typewriter behavior, details-on-demand, auto-follow, unread indicators, and history rendering reuse. ELM-005 should evolve these modules from event-card presentation into conversation-style message rendering instead of introducing a separate renderer stack.

Decision: Reuse extension/src/sidepanel/useRunHistory.ts for history compatibility. ELM-005 does not change history management scope, but the approved design requires live and history views to reuse the same conversation-style presentation so that the interaction model stays consistent across active and past runs.

Decision: Reject extension/src/shared/protocol.ts, extension/src/shared/api.ts, extension/src/background/index.ts, backend/src/**, and python_adapter/** as primary ownership modules for ELM-005. Existing NormalizedRunEvent and SSE contracts already provide enough information for front-end mapping, and the formal requirement explicitly excludes backend capability or protocol redesign. Therefore the change remains an extension-side presentation and orchestration refinement.
